ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: Evil always, and recalcitrant, with a stiff neck ye wish not that ye should be overcome; thus ye will be heirs.
Isaiah said that ye were of hardened heart.
Ye look upon the law which Moses in wrath dashed to pieces; and the same Lord gave to him a second law.
In that he placed his hope; but ye, half healed, reject it, and therefore ye shall not be worthy of the kingdom of heaven.
